statement: Compute 21 + 73 + 38. Give your final answer as a single number.
Compute 21 + 21 + 48. Give your final answer as a single number.
Start with 112, subtract 43, then add 15. Give your final answer as a single number.
Start with 178, subtract 46, then add 53. Give your final answer as a single number.
What is 10 times 8, minus 27? Give your final answer as a single number.
What is 10 times 18, minus 28? Give your final answer as a single number.
Compute 3/7 + 2/5. Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
Compute 8/11 + 4/9. Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
Find the average of 76, 30, 10, 28, and 77. Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
Find the average of 38, 60, 32, 80, and 24. Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
There are 4 groups of 9 and 9 groups of 15. How many items are there? Give your final answer as a single number.
There are 11 groups of 23 and 3 groups of 20. How many items are there? Give your final answer as a single number.
Compute (9 + 17) times (14 - 5) plus 20. Give your final answer as a single number.
Compute (15 + 19) times (23 - 10) plus 18. Give your final answer as a single number.
A value starts at 1000. It increases by 40% and then decreases by 10%. What is the final value? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
A value starts at 180. It increases by 50% and then decreases by 40%. What is the final value? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
Begin with 160. Use 1/3 of it, then use 1/4 of what remains. How much is left? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
Begin with 127. Use 1/8 of it, then use 1/4 of what remains. How much is left? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
What is the greatest common divisor of 128 and 115? Give your final answer as a single number.
What is the greatest common divisor of 121 and 82? Give your final answer as a single number.
What is the least common multiple of 10 and 24? Give your final answer as a single number.
What is the least common multiple of 28 and 13? Give your final answer as a single number.
How many multiples of 8 are between 59 and 211, inclusive? Give your final answer as a single number.
How many multiples of 14 are between 19 and 106, inclusive? Give your final answer as a single number.
What is the remainder when 20 times 7 plus 3 is divided by 24? Give your final answer as a single number.
What is the remainder when 18 times 14 plus 8 is divided by 8? Give your final answer as a single number.
What is the smallest nonnegative integer x such that 14 + x is divisible by 18? Give your final answer as a single number.
What is the smallest nonnegative integer x such that 38 + x is divisible by 9? Give your final answer as a single number.
Let n = 11^2 times 13^3. What is the sum of all positive divisors of n? Give your final answer as a single number.
Let n = 2^3 times 13^3. What is the sum of all positive divisors of n? Give your final answer as a single number.
If n has prime factorization 2^1 times 3^1 times 5^1, how many positive divisors does n have? Give your final answer as a single number.
If n has prime factorization 2^2 times 3^4 times 5^3, how many positive divisors does n have? Give your final answer as a single number.
Let n = 11 times 7, where both factors are prime. How many positive integers less than n are relatively prime to n? Give your final answer as a single number.
Let n = 11 times 5, where both factors are prime. How many positive integers less than n are relatively prime to n? Give your final answer as a single number.
What is the least nonnegative integer x such that x leaves remainder 6 when divided by 7 and remainder 4 when divided by 8? Give your final answer as a single number.
What is the least nonnegative integer x such that x leaves remainder 3 when divided by 7 and remainder 3 when divided by 11? Give your final answer as a single number.
Solve for x: 6x + 24 = 36. Give your final answer as a single number.
Solve for x: 9x + 4 = 148. Give your final answer as a single number.
Solve for x: 6x - 18 = 42. Give your final answer as a single number.
Solve for x: 7x - 31 = -17. Give your final answer as a single number.
Solve for x: x/6 + 15 = 25. Give your final answer as a single number.
Solve for x: x/8 + 4 = 21. Give your final answer as a single number.
An arithmetic sequence has first term -12 and common difference 3. What is term 28? Give your final answer as a single number.
An arithmetic sequence has first term 24 and common difference 13. What is term 26? Give your final answer as a single number.
A geometric sequence has first term 8 and common ratio 3. What is term 6? Give your final answer as a single number.
A geometric sequence has first term 4 and common ratio 3. What is term 6? Give your final answer as a single number.
Solve for x: 2x + 4y = -12 and 7x + 3y = 13. Give your final answer as a single number.
Solve for x: 4x + 8y = 56 and 6x + 1y = 62. Give your final answer as a single number.
The equation x^2 - 29x + 168 = 0 has two positive integer roots. What is the larger root? Give your final answer as a single number.
The equation x^2 - 29x + 180 = 0 has two positive integer roots. What is the larger root? Give your final answer as a single number.
Let f(t) = 7t + 18 and g(t) = 7t + 2. What is f(g(-3))? Give your final answer as a single number.
Let f(t) = 2t + 1 and g(t) = 3t + -6. What is f(g(-2))? Give your final answer as a single number.
Solve for x: 4 times (x - 17) / 4 = 6. Give your final answer as a single number.
Solve for x: 3 times (x - 4) / 3 = 9. Give your final answer as a single number.
A menu has 8 toppings. How many ways are there to choose 2 different toppings? Give your final answer as a single number.
A menu has 9 toppings. How many ways are there to choose 2 different toppings? Give your final answer as a single number.
From 7 students, choose a committee of 5 and then choose one committee member as chair. How many outcomes are possible? Give your final answer as a single number.
From 10 students, choose a committee of 3 and then choose one committee member as chair. How many outcomes are possible? Give your final answer as a single number.
A code uses 3 distinct letters chosen from 6 available letters, and order matters. How many codes are possible? Give your final answer as a single number.
A code uses 2 distinct letters chosen from 5 available letters, and order matters. How many codes are possible? Give your final answer as a single number.
How many distinct arrangements can be made from 2 A letters, 3 B letters, and 2 C letters? Give your final answer as a single number.
How many distinct arrangements can be made from 1 A letters, 3 B letters, and 1 C letters? Give your final answer as a single number.
On a grid, move only right or up from (0, 0) to (4, 5). How many paths pass through (2, 1)? Give your final answer as a single number.
On a grid, move only right or up from (0, 0) to (6, 6). How many paths pass through (3, 2)? Give your final answer as a single number.
How many ways can 13 identical tokens be placed into 5 labeled boxes if each box gets at least 2? Give your final answer as a single number.
How many ways can 16 identical tokens be placed into 4 labeled boxes if each box gets at least 3? Give your final answer as a single number.
From positions 1 through 9, how many ways can 3 positions be chosen with no two chosen positions adjacent? Give your final answer as a single number.
From positions 1 through 6, how many ways can 2 positions be chosen with no two chosen positions adjacent? Give your final answer as a single number.
How many strings of length 6 over the alphabet A, B, C contain at least one A and at least one B? Give your final answer as a single number.
How many strings of length 6 over the alphabet A, B, C contain at least one A and at least one B? Give your final answer as a single number.
How many functions from a 5-element set onto a 2-element set are there? Give your final answer as a single number.
How many functions from a 4-element set onto a 4-element set are there? Give your final answer as a single number.
A fair six-sided die is rolled once. What is the probability of rolling at least 6? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
A fair six-sided die is rolled once. What is the probability of rolling at least 3? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
A bag has 3 red, 5 blue, and 5 green marbles. One marble is drawn. What is the probability it is red? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
A bag has 5 red, 6 blue, and 2 green marbles. One marble is drawn. What is the probability it is red? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
Two fair six-sided dice are rolled. What is the probability that their sum is 5? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
Two fair six-sided dice are rolled. What is the probability that their sum is 7? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
A fair coin is flipped 7 times. What is the probability of getting exactly 3 heads? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
A fair coin is flipped 8 times. What is the probability of getting exactly 2 heads? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
A bag has 6 red and 8 blue marbles. Two marbles are drawn without replacement. What is the probability they are the same color? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
A bag has 3 red and 8 blue marbles. Two marbles are drawn without replacement. What is the probability they are the same color? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.

A bag has 3 red, 2 blue, and 3 green marbles. Two marbles are drawn without replacement. What is the probability the first is red and the second is blue? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.

A bag has 2 red, 7 blue, and 5 green marbles. Two marbles are drawn without replacement. What is the probability the first is red and the second is blue? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
A fair six-sided die is rolled 2 times. What is the probability that at least one roll is a 6? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
A fair six-sided die is rolled 3 times. What is the probability that at least one roll is a 6? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
A binary string of length 5 is chosen uniformly at random. What is the probability that it has no adjacent 1s? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
A binary string of length 8 is chosen uniformly at random. What is the probability that it has no adjacent 1s? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
A rectangle has length 37 and width 13. What is its area? Give your final answer as a single number.
A rectangle has length 11 and width 20. What is its area? Give your final answer as a single number.
A triangle has base 28 and height 22. What is its area? Give your final answer as a single number.
A triangle has base 16 and height 30. What is its area? Give your final answer as a single number.
A trapezoid has bases 11 and 21 and height 18. What is its area? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
A trapezoid has bases 15 and 20 and height 6. What is its area? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
A rectangular prism has side lengths 6, 8, and 3. What is its volume? Give your final answer as a single number.
A rectangular prism has side lengths 13, 8, and 11. What is its volume? Give your final answer as a single number.
Points A(2, 6) and B(4, 1) are in the coordinate plane. What is the squared distance AB^2? Give your final answer as a single number.
Points A(-4, 4) and B(-10, -4) are in the coordinate plane. What is the squared distance AB^2? Give your final answer as a single number.
Two triangles are similar. A side of length 8 corresponds to a side of length 13. What length corresponds to a side of length 13? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
Two triangles are similar. A side of length 4 corresponds to a side of length 15. What length corresponds to a side of length 15? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
A right triangle has hypotenuse 30 and one leg 18. What is the length of the other leg? Give your final answer as a single number.
A right triangle has hypotenuse 125 and one leg 35. What is the length of the other leg? Give your final answer as a single number.
A 22 by 22 rectangle has a 6 by 5 rectangular corner removed. What area remains? Give your final answer as a single number.
A 29 by 16 rectangle has a 7 by 7 rectangular corner removed. What area remains? Give your final answer as a single number.
Emery rides from the maker space at 39 miles per hour for 6 hours. How many miles are covered? Give your final answer as a single number.
Finley rides from the community lab at 35 miles per hour for 3 hours. How many miles are covered? Give your final answer as a single number.
Avery buys 9 sample jars for a total of 198 dollars. What is the cost of one item? Give your final answer as a single number.
Emery buys 8 poster prints for a total of 32 dollars. What is the cost of one item? Give your final answer as a single number.

Morgan can label a batch of sensor kits in 9 hours. Taylor can label the same batch in 11 hours. Working together, how many hours will they take? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.

Finley can label a batch of circuit boards in 4 hours. Riley can label the same batch in 5 hours. Working together, how many hours will they take? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
Devon and Morgan start 132 miles apart and walk toward each other at 3 and 3 miles per hour. How many hours until they meet? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
Devon and Avery start 360 miles apart and walk toward each other at 4 and 8 miles per hour. How many hours until they meet? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
At the robotics club, a cutter makes 23 notebooks per hour for 5 hours, then 30 per hour for 2 hours. How many are made? Give your final answer as a single number.
At the field station, a cutter makes 29 poster prints per hour for 5 hours, then 27 per hour for 4 hours. How many are made? Give your final answer as a single number.

A pump can fill a tank in 3 hours, while a leak can drain a full tank in 12 hours. If both are active from empty, how many hours does filling take? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.

A pump can fill a tank in 9 hours, while a leak can drain a full tank in 18 hours. If both are active from empty, how many hours does filling take? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.

Finley travels 32 miles out at 40 miles per hour and returns the same distance at 43 miles per hour. What is the average speed for the whole trip? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.

Taylor travels 53 miles out at 54 miles per hour and returns the same distance at 49 miles per hour. What is the average speed for the whole trip? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.

Emery leaves the garden co-op at 8 miles per hour. 4 hours later, Casey leaves from the same place at 13 miles per hour. How many hours after Casey starts will it take to catch up? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.

Avery leaves the robotics club at 5 miles per hour. 5 hours later, Finley leaves from the same place at 10 miles per hour. How many hours after Finley starts will it take to catch up? Give your final answer as a single number or fraction.
